free_text: Excitation functions of high-energy He3 and alpha-
particle induced nuclear reactions on natural krypton
with special reference to the production of Sr-82
Experimental technique and data evaluation
Gas handling system
Typical estimated error of the energy scale at the
upper end ( 100 MeV) of the scale is +-0.3 MeV,
+- 1.0 MeV at the middle (50 MeV) and at the lower
end (25 MeV)  of the scale is +-1.5 MeV.
By authors:
The gas cells, the irradiation arrangement, beam
current and beam profile measurements, radiochemical
separation, sample preparation, gamma-ray spectroscopy
and data evaluation were identical or similar to
F.Tarkanyi et al, J, ARI,39,135,1988.
Gas cells were filled with natural Kr gas.
Primary energy of He-3 particles: 100 MeV, and 120 MeV
for alpha particles.
Total error (12-15%) derived as the
square root of the sum of square of the individual
errors. Main errors:
Disintegration rate (10%)
Beam current <10%
Private communication
BIB section upgraded
On. ERR-ANALYS: Coded information added
(by authors).The amount of produced Sr-82 was
determined by detecting the 776 keV gamma-ray
following the decay of Rb-82-g daughter nuclei, the
contribution from the decay of Rb-82-m was separated
by using decay curve analysis.
(by authors).For determination of the area of the 514
keV gamma line special stripping technique was used.
(by authors).The cross section contains the
contribution from the decay of Sr-83
